diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Opsoclonus-myoclonus syndrome (OMS) secondary to small-cell lung cancer (SCLC)
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was supported by the patient's symptoms of multivectorial eye movements, involuntary multifocal myoclonus, and cerebellar ataxia, along with the presence of a left hilar mass confirmed as SCLC via endobronchial ultrasound and transbronchial needle aspiration. Brain 18-FDG-PET showed diffuse cerebral hypometabolism with cerebellar hypermetabolism, a reported finding in paraneoplastic neurologic disorders (PND). Although standard serological screening for anti-neuronal antibodies was negative, positive anti-GAD antibodies further supported the diagnosis.
